text: Energy Pedals II is a harder iteration of Energy Pedals I. You will be given the same format and field of view as the first exercise. However, you will require much more coordination with the left and right arm and their corresponding foot pedals.

The field of view shows an object with three parts: two barbell-shaped portions and a central sphere. The surgeon uses bipolar coagulation on the barbell portions and performs monopolar coagulation or cutting on the sphere as instructed.

The objects appear randomly in the field of view, requiring the surgeon to use wrist articulation to orient the grasper for grabbing the barbell portions and position the scissors tips to touch the sphere for electrocautery application.
The surgeon uses electrocautery on the object, causing visible smoke to rise from it. As the object turns green, the surgeon releases the foot pedal and prepares to apply cautery to the next section.

As mentioned in energy pedals one, we want to draw attention to the interface, which allows you to orient yourself with your foot position and which pedal you are hovering over. At this moment, the interface is indicating that my right foot is over the left coagulation pedal, and I am now pressing it. Subsequently, you can see the interface shift to indicate that I'm now pressing the right coagulation pedal.

It may take a few tries to get comfortable adapting to the interface and using the right foot pedals, but ultimately that is the goal of this exercise. Looking down to see where the location of your foot is in relation to the foot pedals is suboptimal.
Penalties on this exercise can be incurred when over-quaterizing an area, pressing the wrong foot pedal, and instrument collisions.
